task: v146 preference reply context first stage

module_location: src/jarvis_lite/preferences.py manages saved preferences, confirmation records, history and undo helpers.
src/jarvis_lite/agent.py routes ordinary input through InnerBrain, knowledge answer, LLM fallback, then memory fallback.
src/jarvis_lite/llm.py builds provider instructions and receives Agent-provided context lines.
current_behavior: 0.140.0 writes confirmation audit records only after /preference-apply-confirm succeeds.
/preference-apply-undo marks a confirmation record as undone without changing saved preferences.
_llm_context_lines currently includes memory, recent document/search/directory/advice context, but not preference confirmations.
LLM_ALLOWED_COMMAND_SPECS intentionally excludes preference management commands.
similar_patterns: Recent document/search/directory context is appended to _llm_context_lines without changing provider instructions.
Runtime context preservation in JarvisAgent._runtime_context keeps persisted recent_preference_applications.
Provider instruction whitelist tests in tests/test_llm.py guard against exposing management commands to LLM command routing.
tests: tests/test_preferences.py covers confirmation history, undo and conflict rejection.
tests/test_agent.py covers Agent slash commands and LLM fallback with injected fake providers.

key_questions.question: What qualifies a confirmation as active for ordinary reply context?
key_questions.answer: Only the newest confirmation record may be active; it must be status=confirmed, and its preference IDs/text must exactly match the currently enabled preference set. Any undo, disable, deletion or enabled-set change requires a new confirmation.

key_questions.question: Where should the first ordinary reply integration happen?
key_questions.answer: Use Agent _llm_context_lines, because it is the existing bounded channel for ordinary LLM fallback context and does not alter provider instructions, SearchRouter, InnerBrain or desktop execution policy.

key_questions.question: Should local knowledge or memory fallback rewrite answers directly?
key_questions.answer: No for this first stage. Rewriting local answers would require a separate formatter contract. This stage exposes confirmed preferences to ordinary LLM fallback context and the LLM context preview only.

acceptance: After enabling preferences and confirming them, /llm-context-preview includes a confirmed preference application context.
An ordinary LLM fallback call receives the same confirmed preference context lines.
Undoing the latest confirmation removes preference context from subsequent LLM fallback calls.
Changing enabled preferences after confirmation invalidates the old confirmation context until reconfirmed.
Provider instructions still do not list preference management commands.
Version is bumped to 0.141.0 and docs/verification are updated.
